channel.title: Promaster on Budget Van Life Affiliate Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Promaster on Budget Van Life Affiliate Guide

item.title: Best Navigation Systems for Camper Vans (What We Used + What's Better Now)
item.description:
Best Navigation Systems for Camper Vans (What We Used + What’s Better Now)
#

I spent seven years full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500, and one underrated upgrade was replacing the factory head unit with a more capable navigation and media system. Good route planning, better audio integration, and smoother device connectivity improve daily drive quality and reduce friction on long travel days. This review covers the Kenwood DNX574S from that real-use perspective.

item.title: Compustar CS6900-AS 2-Way Remote Start Security System Review
item.description:
Compustar CS6900-AS 2-Way Remote Start Security System Review
#

After seven years full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500, I stopped thinking about security as an optional add-on. A van is your home, office, and gear locker. A better security system and reliable remote start for camper van use can reduce stress and improve daily convenience in ways most builders underestimate.

item.title: CRL T-Vent Windows for ProMaster Review
item.description:
CRL T-Vent Windows for ProMaster Review
#

I lived in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500 full-time for seven years, and windows were one of the upgrades that most improved daily quality of life. Better airflow, natural light, and less cave-like interior feel can change how long you can comfortably stay inside your van. This review covers CRL T-Vent windows from a practical full-time-use perspective, not a showroom perspective.

item.title: MaxxAir 4500K Fan Review: Budget-Friendly Ventilation for Van Life
item.description:
MaxxAir 4500K Fan Review: Budget-Friendly Ventilation for Van Life
#

I spent seven years living full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500, and one lesson never changed: ventilation is foundational. It is easy to overspend on gadgets and underspend on airflow. The MaxxAir 4500K is often considered the value option in this category, so I tested it as a real daily-use solution and compared it with higher-end alternatives.

item.title: MaxxAir MaxxFan Deluxe 7500K Review: Best Fan for Van Life?
item.description:
MaxxAir MaxxFan Deluxe 7500K Review: Best Fan for Van Life?
#

After seven years full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500, I consider roof ventilation non-negotiable. A quality fan affects sleep quality, condensation control, cooking comfort, and even how stuffy the van feels by afternoon. I tested multiple fan setups over time, and this review focuses on the MaxxAir MaxxFan Deluxe 7500K from a real full-time-use perspective.

item.title: Renogy 100W Slim Solar Panels Review: 3-Panel Stealth ProMaster Setup
item.description:
Renogy 100W Slim Solar Panels Review: 3-Panel Stealth ProMaster Setup
#

I lived full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500 for seven years, and roof solar was one of the most important systems I dialed in. My goal was not flashy max wattage. My goal was a stealth solar van setup that looked low profile, survived weather, and delivered consistent daily charging. This review covers a practical 3-panel Renogy 100W slim configuration and how it performs in real van use.

item.title: Van Life Insulation Guide: Radiant Technology Composite + Polyiso
item.description:
Van Life Insulation Guide: Radiant Technology Composite + Polyiso
#

I lived full-time in a 2017 Dodge ProMaster 2500 for seven years, and insulation was one of the biggest “do it right once” decisions in my build. Bad insulation choices create permanent comfort issues, condensation risk, and wasted power draw from fans/heaters. This review-guide covers a practical approach using radiant composite layers plus polyiso in a real van build context.
